sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: 메드트로닉은 2026 회계연도 매출 363억 6,400만 달러를 기록하며 심혈관 포트폴리오의 혁신 제품군을 중심으로 성장을 지속하고 있습니다.

keyPoints.text: 심혈관 부문에서 Micra 페이싱 포트폴리오와 PulseSelect, Affera 등 전기생리학(EP) 혁신 제품의 글로벌 채택이 성장을 견인하고 있습니다.
GAAP 기준 순이익은 48억 100만 달러를 기록했으며, 무형자산 상각비 17억 7,200만 달러 등 비경상적 조정 항목이 실적에 영향을 미쳤습니다.
글로벌 시장에서의 기술적 우위 확보를 위해 연구개발 및 임상 개발 분야에서 경쟁사 및 학술 기관과 치열한 인재 확보 경쟁을 벌이고 있습니다.

riskChanges.text: 기후 변화로 인한 자연재해는 제조 및 유통 네트워크를 포함한 공급망 전반에 물리적 위험을 초래하고 운영 비용을 상승시킬 수 있습니다.
특허 보호 만료 이후 경쟁사의 개선된 제품 출시나 대체 의료 요법의 등장은 기존 제품의 시장 점유율을 잠식할 위험이 있습니다.

businessChanges.text: 심혈관 사업부의 성장을 위해 Aurora EV-ICD 및 Cobalt/Crome 포트폴리오의 글로벌 확장을 전략적으로 추진하고 있습니다.
2026 회계연도 영업이익은 64억 6,700만 달러로, 구조조정 비용 및 인수 관련 항목들이 재무 성과에 반영되었습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 2026 회계연도 매출은 363억 6,400만 달러, 총자산은 930억 2,800만 달러 규모를 유지하고 있습니다.
현금및현금성자산은 19억 4,900만 달러로, 안정적인 운영을 위한 유동성을 확보하고 있습니다.

watchItems.text: Affera 시스템 등 신규 승인된 혁신 의료기기의 시장 침투 속도가 향후 매출총이익률 개선과 시장 지배력 강화의 핵심 지표가 될 것입니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: FY2026 operating income = 64억 6,700만 달러 (USD 6.467B; 6,467 million), XBRL range 2025-04-26..2026-04-24.
FY2026 net income = 48억 100만 달러 (USD 4.801B; 4,801 million), XBRL range 2025-04-26..2026-04-24.
FY2026 total assets = 930억 2,800만 달러 (USD 93.028B; 93,028 million), XBRL range instant..2026-04-24.
FY2026 cash and cash equivalents = 19억 4,900만 달러 (USD 1.949B; 1,949 million), XBRL range instant..2026-04-24.

Operational Risks We operate in a highly competitive industry and we may be unable to compete effectively. We compete in both the therapeutic and diagnostic medical markets in more than 150 countries throughout the world. These markets are characterized by rapid change resulting from technological advances, innovations and scientific discoveries. Our product lines face a mix of competitors ranging from large companies with multiple business lines to small, specialized manufacturers that offer a limited selection of niche products. Development by other companies of new or improved products, processes, technologies, or the introduction of reprocessed products or competitive devices when our proprietary products lose their patent protection may make our existing or planned products less competitive. We also face competition from providers of alternative medical therapies, such as pharmaceut

Competition may increase as additional companies enter our markets or modify their existing products to compete more directly with ours. In addition, academic institutions, governmental agencies and other public and private research organizations may conduct research, seek patent protection and establish collaborative arrangements for discovery, research, clinical development and marketing of products similar to ours. These companies and institutions compete with us in recruiting and retaining qualified scientific and management personnel, as well as in acquiring necessary product technologies. From time to time, we have lost, and may in the future lose, market share in connection with product problems, physician advisories, safety alerts, publications about our products, or the introduction of competing technologies perceived to offer improved clinical, workflow, or economic outcomes, w

We face current and long-term operational risks and have in the past experienced business interruptions from severe weather events and other natural conditions, such as hurricanes, tornadoes, droughts, extreme temperatures, wildfires or flooding. Such severe weather events caused by or related to climate change or other conditions caused by natural disasters have in the past and could in the future increase our operational costs, pose physical risks to our facilities and adversely impact our supply chain, including: manufacturing and distribution networks, the availability and cost of raw materials and components, energy supply, transportation, or other inputs necessary for the operation of our business. The impacts of climate change on global water resources may result in water scarcity, which could impact our ability to access sufficient quantities of water in certain locations and res

Overview, looking ahead, we expect Cardiovascular could be affected by the following: • Continued global penetration of our Micra transcatheter pacing portfolio. • Continued acceptance and growth of the 3830 lead. • Global adoption and growth of Aurora EV-ICD. • Growth of the Cobalt and Crome portfolio of ICDs and CRT-Ds. • Continued growth and utilization of the TYRX Envelope for implantable devices. • Continued use and acceptance of Reveal LINQ and expansion of the LINQ II cardiac monitor. • Continued acceptance, adoption, and growth of our innovative portfolio of products in the electrophysiology (EP) segment, including the PulseSelect pulsed field ablation system and the Affera mapping and ablation system with Sphere-9 catheter.
